LEVEL '''{{{no|918}}}'''
SURVIVAL DIFFICULTY
'''CLASS Ψ'''

Devoid of Life
Level 918, the 919th level of the Backrooms, is a level where nostalgia and creepiness intertwine.
'''DESCRIPTION'''
[[File:914image1.jpg|517x517px|center|link=]]
Level 918, colloquially known as "'''The Museum of Distorted Memories'''" is a prison of nostalgia. It alters and changes the thoughts and feelings of one's own mind, twisting and bending pleasant memories into disturbing fragments of the past. As the name of the level suggests, the level takes on the appearance of a grand and immaculate museum, with an abundance of pictures hung on the walls as well as stone statues that are placed in glass chambers, creating both an immersive and unsettling experience for those who dare to enter the level. The most noticeable thing in the level is the items that lie within the museum. For starters, most of the objects and artwork in the museum are weirdcore, giving off an extremely unsettling vibe that can cause hallucinations and may even lead one to turn into an [[Phenomenon 35|Insanity]]. The strange artwork contains seemingly familiar scenery as if it was a depiction of something that happened in your life, giving off a sense of nostalgia as if the level knows the identity of the wanderer. The statues at first glance may seem sentient, onemay notice the blinking of eyes or the movement of the mouth, but this is merely just a trick of the eye. Reports have shown that the statues' appearance may vary depending on whoever entered the level but the statues almost always resemble the friends and family of the wanderer that entered, which may cause one to get emotional.
'''The Distortion Event''' If one explores the museum long enough, onemay trigger an anomaly called "The Distortion Event". This anomaly can be triggered at any moment and only targets specific people, mostly those with a dark past. The first thing one may notice is that the level gets a lot darker and that the atmosphere becomes extremely unsettling. The statues will start moving, attempting to break out of their motionless trance. The once vibrant and nostalgic pictures and artifacts start to become twisted. As The Distortion Event intensifies, the walls will start to pulsate with seemingly otherworldly energy. Everything in the museum starts to become distorted and macabre. Dark red liquid starts to appear on the paintings, resembling bloodstains. The artifacts in the level start to ooze out a strange black liquid. Touching the liquid will inflict painful burns and may even lead to the complete loss of skin and flesh in the affected area. Soon The Distortion Event will become dangerous to one's mental health. Wanderers often see hallucinations of their family and friends covered in blood when in the midst of The Distortion Event. Their sanity will slowly start to crumble from the weight of the wanderer's regrets and bad decisions. In the end, the memories of the wanderer will fade away as they become lost and forgotten, and the level will revert back to its regular state. It's strongly recommended not to stay in the level for too long and escape the level as fast as one can to minimize the risk of accidentally triggering The Distortion Event.
'''ENTITIES'''
Currently, there are no living documented entities in '''Level 918''', though the level itself could be considered an entity because of its mind-altering abilities.
'''SETTLEMENTS'''
It is not possible for settlements to exist in the vast depths of '''Level 918''', as only one wanderer can enter at a time. If two wanderers try to enter the level at the same exact time, only one of them will enter, whilst the other person will not be able to enter, as if a mysterious force were stopping the person from entering the level.
'''ADDENDUM'''
{{Collapse|num=132124124124|initial=Open Discovery Log?|final=Close Discovery Log?|body=
My wanderings have brought me to a purgatorial realm of memories. The past has always been something I've managed to avoid for years. But the return was inevitable. Resurfacing memories brought me back to the Incidents. My previous sins have always managed to catch up with me, regardless of what plane of existence I'm on. This place, however, had the one ability that I had always feared: the distortion and twisting of memories. My past was one of immeasurable cruelty, and the altering of these memories made it hard to differentiate. Blood-splattered images of my family. Pictures of everyone I know gone. Dead. Missing. Removed from the world. Fortunately, the database had no level even similar to this one, so it seems I've found a new one.
}}
'''ENTRANCES AND EXITS'''

'''Entrances''' *One can access the level by unlocking the door in the basement of any [[Level 912]]'s houses. However, this doesn't apply to [[Level 912]]'s entities. *It is speculated that going through every level (except Level 918) can cause one to teleport here. *Some say sitting in [[Level 922]] for an hour can lead one to this level. *One can access this level from [[Level 917|the previous one]] by traversing a pathway leading away from the main parking lot.
'''Exits''' *Surviving The Distortion Event without suffering from memory loss or insanity can cause one to be teleported to [[Level 699]]. *Noclipping through a painting during The Distortion Event can lead one to [[The Void]]. *Staying in the level for a day without triggering The Distortion Event will lead one back to [[Level 912]]. *Noclipping through a painting depicting the Poolrooms will lead one to [[Level 919]]. However, this doesn't work during the Distortion Event.
